title: Fix Vtrigprevexpr type does not match signal type (#5125)

features.name: zero_width_unpacked_array_1bit_signal
features.description: Code should include a 1-bit signal declared as a single-element unpacked array using zero-width range (e.g., bit [0:0] or logic [0:0])

features.name: unpacked_array_neq_comparison
features.description: Code should include a single-element unpacked array of a 1-bit signal used in a comparison operation that triggers type mismatch in Vtrigprevexpr generation
features.snippet:
byte unsigned arr[1];
initial if (arr[0] != 0) begin end

features.name: single_element_unpacked_array_assignment
features.description: Code should include a 1-bit signal declared as a single-element unpacked array being assigned or used in a context where VlUnpacked type is expected

extraction_summary.notes: PR #5131 fixes issue #5125. The bug involves C++ code generation where 1-bit signals declared as zero-width unpacked arrays (e.g., bit [0:0]) get incorrect type mapping between CData[1] and VlUnpacked, causing .neq() and .assign() method calls to fail during compilation.
